purpose: Pilot industry source maps for prompt-first industry evidence runs.

industries.pilot_question: For 2026, are North American hyperscaler capex plans still being revised upward, and what evidence links that spend to optical modules, servers, data centers, and networking equipment?

metrics.prompt_role: Extract company-specific capex guidance, actual quarterly capex, server/network/data-center allocation, and management commentary.
metrics.notes: SEC/company IR can be deterministic; supplier read-through remains prompt evidence.

metrics.prompt_role: Capture AI revenue run-rate, backlog, and capacity-constraint comments.
metrics.notes: Often appears in narrative transcript, so extraction should keep raw excerpts and source lines.

metrics.prompt_role: Extract split between servers, networking equipment, and data centers when disclosed.
metrics.notes: Alphabet disclosed server vs data-center/networking allocation in Q1 2026 call; similar disclosures are not guaranteed.

metrics.prompt_role: Find supplier-level order, backlog, customer concentration, or demand commentary.
metrics.notes: Usually non-standard text. Keep as prompt evidence until repeat source patterns emerge.

metrics.prompt_role: Use association releases to cross-check AI hardware cycle and semiconductor equipment demand.
metrics.notes: Confirm public/free data availability before adapter work.

first_run_evidence.claim: Microsoft said AI business ARR surpassed $37 billion and grew 123% in FY26 Q3.
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Strong AI revenue supports continued AI infrastructure demand.

first_run_evidence.claim: Microsoft FY26 Q2 capex was $37.5 billion, roughly two thirds short-lived assets primarily GPUs and CPUs; demand continued to exceed supply.
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Direct demand signal for compute hardware and surrounding infrastructure.

first_run_evidence.claim: Alphabet Q1 2026 capex was $35.7 billion, mostly technical infrastructure; about 60% was servers and 40% data centers and networking equipment.
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Explicit server/networking allocation supports supplier-chain read-through.

first_run_evidence.claim: Meta raised 2026 capex guidance to $125-145 billion, citing component pricing and data-center costs.
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Capex guidance revision is direct evidence of larger AI/data-center buildout.

first_run_evidence.claim: Amazon said its expected 2026 AWS capex is backed by customer commitments and referenced approximately $200 billion of 2026 capex.
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Management links AI/cloud capex to customer demand, reducing risk that capex is purely speculative.

gaps.missing: Supplier-specific optical-module and server order values
gaps.suggested_source: Supplier filings, IR transcripts, tender notices, and official interaction platforms.

industries.pilot_question: Since 2026, do lithium carbonate inventory, energy-storage tenders, power-battery installation, and NEV sales support a demand recovery in the lithium battery chain?

metrics.prompt_role: Use exchange evidence to anchor lithium carbonate inventory or warehouse-receipt changes.
metrics.notes: Exchange data is a strong deterministic adapter candidate.

metrics.prompt_role: Use futures price as a public market proxy when high-quality spot prices are paid or unavailable.
metrics.notes: Spot prices from paid vendors should remain prompt/manual unless licensed.

metrics.prompt_role: Find original power-battery installation releases and separate official source from reposts.
metrics.notes: Media reposts are medium confidence until original source path is verified.

metrics.prompt_role: Anchor battery demand with NEV production and sales.
metrics.notes: CAAM/statistics articles should be parsed only after format stability review.

metrics.prompt_role: Discover energy-storage EPC, cell framework purchase, and award signals; backtrack aggregators to official notice pages.
metrics.notes: Needs dedupe, project-size extraction, unit conversion, and notice-type classification.

metrics.prompt_role: Identify public snippets but avoid production numeric use without licensed source.
metrics.notes: High-quality spot sources are often SMM/Mysteel/Longzhong paid or semi-paid.

first_run_evidence.claim: GFEX publishes daily warehouse receipts and showed lithium carbonate subtotal values on its warehouse-receipt page.
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Public exchange inventory proxy for lithium carbonate.

first_run_evidence.claim: GFEX lithium carbonate contract specs define the underlying, trading unit, and RMB/ton quotation unit.
first_run_evidence.source_name: Guangzhou Futures Exchange lithium carbonate product page
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Supports unit normalization for price and inventory evidence.

first_run_evidence.claim: Power-battery installation data requires an original association or official-platform source before it can support production evidence.
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Demand-side improvement can be important, but reposted values are not accepted as evidence.

first_run_evidence.claim: Auto Statistics Information Network published April 2026 NEV production and sales totals and year-to-date totals.
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Monthly NEV demand anchor for battery-chain analysis.

first_run_evidence.claim: National public-resource transaction platforms provide searchable tender and award information across government procurement and engineering projects.
first_run_evidence.why_it_matters: Can support energy-storage tender and award evidence when project notices are matched.

gaps.missing: High-quality free lithium carbonate spot price
gaps.suggested_source: SMM, Mysteel, Longzhong, or licensed vendor feed.

gaps.missing: Stable original source for monthly power-battery installation data
gaps.suggested_source: China Automotive Power Battery Industry Innovation Alliance original publication path.
